title: Hybrid Search
learning_outcomes: Explain Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) scoring mathematics.
Evaluate rank aggregation challenges for disparate similarity distributions.

content_sections.title: Lexical-Dense Fusion Challenges
content_sections.content: Hybrid search combines sparse keyword retrieval (e.g. BM25) and dense semantic vector retrieval. However, raw score distributions differ significantly: BM25 outputs unbounded positive scores, while dense retrieval outputs bounded similarities ($[-1, 1]$). Direct normalization is unstable.

content_sections.title: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) Mathematics
content_sections.content:
To resolve this, we rank documents using **Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)**. RRF evaluates document ranks rather than raw scores, making it scale-invariant:

$$\text{RRF\_Score}(d \in \mathcal{D}) = \sum_{m \in M} \frac{1}{k + r_m(d)}$$

where $M$ is the set of retrieval models, $r_m(d)$ is the rank of document $d$ under model $m$, and $k \approx 60$ is a constant that prevents top-ranked documents from dominating the scoring metrics.
